title: Song Of Proserpine While Gathering Flowers On The Plain Of Enna.
content:
1.
    Sacred Goddess, Mother Earth,
    Thou from whose immortal bosom
    Gods, and men, and beasts have birth,
    Leaf and blade, and bud and blossom,
    Breathe thine influence most divine
    On thine own child, Proserpine.

    2.
    If with mists of evening dew
    Thou dost nourish these young flowers
    Till they grow, in scent and hue,
    Fairest children of the Hours,
    Breathe thine influence most divine
    On thine own child, Proserpine.
meaning:
"Song Of Proserpine While Gathering Flowers On The Plain Of Enna." is a quintessential example of Percy Bysshe Shelley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
